resources.string: Follow WREK on the web: www.wrek.org

WREK is the entirely student managed, operated and engineered radio station at Georgia Tech in Atlanta. WREK broadcasts 24 hours a day from the Georgia Tech Student Center on 91.1 FM with 100,000 watts throughout Atlanta. The station also broadcasts a diversity of its current programming on an HD subchannel. The station offers its air signal online in addition to a two week mp3 archive of all recorded shows.

WREK has been broadcasting since March 25th, 1968. Broadcasting from the top of the Van Leer Electrical Engineering building, the first song played over the air was "Spooky" by the Classics IV. Years later, the station moved to the offices adjacent to the Alexander Memorial Coliseum where it stayed until 2004 when it moved to the newly remodelled Student Center.

Since then, names, faces, and clothing fashions may have changed, but the station-wide philosophy of providing diverse, quality programming to the Atlanta area and the world at large has remained unwavering.

In 1994, WREK became one of the first radio stations to stream its air signal over the Internet.
